notes: Use aoa-adr-write when the task is to capture a concrete architectural or workflow decision.
Use aoa-source-of-truth-check when the task is to identify which documents are authoritative and reduce overlap.
aoa-bounded-context-map is about naming contexts and interfaces at the system or domain level.
aoa-core-logic-boundary is about separating reusable rules from glue or orchestration.
aoa-port-adapter-refactor is about isolating a concrete dependency behind a port or adapter.
aoa-change-protocol is the generic bounded-change workflow.
aoa-tdd-slice is specifically test-first feature slicing.
aoa-contract-test is specifically about validating a module/service/workflow boundary.
aoa-property-invariants authors new invariant or property checks.
aoa-invariant-coverage-audit inspects whether current tests already prove the relevant invariants.
aoa-approval-gate-check classifies risk and whether execution is allowed at all.
aoa-dry-run-first insists on preview or simulation before a real mutation.
aoa-safe-infra-change handles bounded infra or config changes with rollback thinking.
aoa-local-stack-bringup is the local multi-service stack start path and is explicit-only.
aoa-sanitized-share is about turning raw technical material into a safe public artifact.
Use the ATM10 overlays only when the repository is actually an atm10-* repo and repo-relative overlays matter.
Use the base AoA skill when the workflow does not need a thin project overlay.

cases.prompt: Review the current test suite and tell me whether it actually proves the important invariants for this parser, then name the thinnest gaps.
cases.note: This is an audit of existing coverage, not authoring new properties.

cases.prompt: Write invariant-oriented checks for this parser so we verify idempotency and structural preservation across many generated inputs.
cases.note: This authors new property checks instead of auditing existing coverage.

cases.prompt: The terms account, tenant, workspace, and organization are overloaded across this repo. Map the bounded contexts and the interfaces between them before we change code.
cases.note: This is primarily semantic boundary mapping at the context level.

cases.prompt: The discount rules are mixed into orchestration code and cron wiring. Separate reusable business rules from glue without widening the refactor.
cases.note: This is about rule-vs-glue separation, not system context mapping or a dependency seam.

cases.prompt: Our service layer is tightly coupled to the concrete Stripe client. Introduce a narrow port and adapter so tests stop depending on the live SDK shape.
cases.note: This is dependency isolation behind a port and adapter.

cases.prompt: Add the new password-reset behavior test-first: define the expected flow in tests, implement the smallest passing slice, and keep the refactor bounded.
cases.note: This is specifically a test-first feature slice.

cases.prompt: The webhook producer and consumer disagree on payload shape. Add contract-oriented validation so the boundary is explicit and regressions are obvious.
cases.note: Boundary contract validation is the center of gravity here.

cases.prompt: Make a bounded non-trivial repository change across code and docs, verify it, and report exactly what changed and how you checked it.
cases.note: General bounded change workflow, not specifically test-first or infra-heavy.

cases.prompt: Before touching production DNS records, classify whether this request is safe, approval-gated, or out of bounds.
cases.note: Even though the prompt matches the domain strongly, this skill is explicit-only and should require a manual $skill invocation.

cases.prompt: Find the safest preview path for this delete or restore operation, show what the preview proves, and stop before any real mutation.
cases.note: This skill is explicit-only; implicit routing should not auto-select it.

cases.prompt: Plan and verify a bounded Kubernetes configuration change with rollback thinking and proportional checks.
cases.note: Strong semantic match, but implicit routing must stay off because this skill is explicit-only.

cases.prompt: Show what this docker-compose plus background worker stack would start, run host readiness checks, then launch it through one explicit entrypoint.
cases.note: This is an explicit-only operational skill and should not auto-trigger.

cases.prompt: Turn these raw logs and config snippets into a public-safe incident appendix while keeping the raw material separate.
cases.note: This skill is explicit-only because sanitized sharing is safety-sensitive and should be invoked intentionally.

cases.prompt: Inside this atm10-billing repo, apply the base change protocol but use repo-relative atm10 paths, local commands, and local approval notes.
cases.note: The overlay should win only when the repo is actually an atm10-* repo and the project-specific overlay matters.

cases.prompt: Apply the bounded change workflow in this generic service repo. There is no atm10 overlay or project-specific path layer here.
cases.note: The base skill should win when no ATM10 overlay is needed.

cases.prompt: Inside this atm10-orders repo, map the authoritative docs using repo-relative atm10 canonical-file patterns and local review posture.
cases.note: Overlay-specific source-of-truth mapping inside an ATM10 repo.

cases.prompt: This repo has three conflicting architecture guides and two outdated runbooks. Identify which files are authoritative and shorten the entrypoints into a link-driven map.
cases.note: Document authority mapping for a generic repo, not an overlay-specific ATM10 case and not a decision record task.
